column1: # SemanticFlags.tsv -- per-grain semantic vocabulary for EmulatR V4
# Project: EmulatR -- Alpha AXP / EV6 Architecture Emulator (V4)
# Copyright (C) 2025, 2026 eNVy Systems, Inc.  All rights reserved.
# Licensed under eNVy Systems Non-Commercial License v1.1
# Project Architect: Timothy Peer
# AI Collaboration:  Claude (Anthropic)
# Commercial use prohibited without separate license.
# Contact:        peert@envysys.com  |  https://envysys.com
# Documentation:  https://timothypeer.github.io/ASA-EMulatR-Project/
# Purpose
# This file is the second source of truth for the GrainFactory codegen,
# alongside GrainMaster.tsv.  Each row defines one semantic flag bit
# carried on the InstructionGrain.  The codegen tool joins the two TSVs
# at build time and refuses to emit if a GrainMaster row references a
# flag name that is not defined here, or if a flag name is defined here
# but never referenced by any row in GrainMaster.
# Encoding axis (which leaf to call) is owned by the dispatch tables.
# Semantics axis (how the pipeline handles the result) is owned by the
# flags below.  The pipeline never switches on opcode -- it consults
# semFlags at the stage that owns the predicate.
# Format
# Tab-separated.  Lines starting with '#' are comments.  Blank lines
# are ignored.  Column header row is the first non-comment line.
#   bit          integer 0..63 (uint64_t flag word)
#   name         S_xxx identifier referenced by GrainMaster.tsv
#   category     grouping for codegen-emitted enum sections
#   stages       comma-separated list of stages that read this flag
#                  (DE GR EX MEM WB issue trace)
#   description  short prose; no tabs
# Mutex groups are documented in the trailer at the bottom of this
# file and enforced by codegen.
bit
# --- format identification (mutually exclusive: exactly one per grain) ----
# --- operand presence and register file routing ---------------------------

column5: IMM bit set; 8-bit literal replaces Rb
Any source operand reads integer regfile
Any source operand reads FP regfile
Normal cacheable access (default; absence means uncached)
Writes destination register Ra (loads; BSR/JSR write return PC)
Grain may divert PC at EX
Raises OPCDEC if not in PAL mode
CALL_PAL: enters PALcode at dispatch entry
HW_REI: exits PALcode and resumes EXC_ADDR
CALL_PAL function decoded inline; no PAL transfer (CSERVE etc.)
Valid under Alpha Linux PAL personality (per AARM Table C-15 column 3)

column1: # --- floating-point traps and FPCR access ----------------------------------
# --- internal processor register access ------------------------------------

column5: I-cache memory barrier (CALL_PAL IMB); invalidates traces
Excluded from trace cache (HW_REI, RPCC, IPR access)
Reads cycle counter (RPCC); non-deterministic w.r.t. snapshot
No commit; prefetch hint only (FETCH, FETCH_M, ECB)

column1: # Operand position conventions
# Alpha ISA encoding determines the read/write directionality of each
# operand position.  The operand-category flag set is therefore asymmetric:
#   Ra  read or write     S_ReadsRa, S_WritesRa
#                         loads write Ra; stores read Ra; conditional
#                         branches read Ra; BSR/JSR write Ra with return PC
#   Rb  source only       S_ReadsRb
#                         Mem-format base; Op-format second source;
#                         JMP/JSR/RET indirect target.  No Alpha encoding
#                         writes Rb -- S_WritesRb intentionally not defined.
#   Rc  destination only  S_WritesRc
#                         Op-format and Fp-format destination.  No Alpha
#                         encoding reads Rc -- S_ReadsRc intentionally not
#                         defined.
#   lit literal source    S_HasLit
#                         Op-format IMM bit indicates 8-bit literal in
#                         place of Rb.
# Bit 22 holds S_WritesRa despite category drift into the memory-bit
# region; renumbering the file would have rippled through every flag
# above bit 11.  The category column is the source of truth for grouping.
# Mutex groups (codegen-enforced; codegen rejects rows that violate these)
# format-mutex
#   Exactly one of: S_PalFormat S_BraFormat S_MemFormat S_OpFormat
#                   S_FpFormat  S_HwFormat  S_JmpFormat S_MiscFormat
#   Every leaf must declare exactly one format flag.
# memrw-mutex
#   At most one of: S_Load S_Store
#   A single Alpha instruction either loads or stores; STQ_C is a store
#   (S_Store + S_Locked), LDQ_L is a load (S_Load + S_Locked).
# pal-direction-mutex
#   At most one of: S_PalEntry S_PalExit S_PalIntrinsic
#   PalEntry is CALL_PAL with a real PALcode transfer (HALT, BPT, CHME,
#   CHMK).  PalExit is HW_REI leaving PALcode.  PalIntrinsic is a
#   CALL_PAL function decoded inline -- the C++ leaf reads operand
#   regs directly, packs a regfile commit into BoxResult, and retires
#   to PC+4 with no PAL environment entry.  CSERVE is the canonical
#   PalIntrinsic; future synthetic services (debugger hooks, firmware
#   fastpaths, emulator assists) take the same flag.  The three are
#   architecturally distinct posture of CALL_PAL and never overlap on
#   one grain.
# regdest-mutex
#   At most one of: S_WritesInt S_WritesFp
#   A single instruction writes to one register file (or none).
# ipr-mutex
#   At most one of: S_IprRead S_IprWrite
#   HW_MFPR reads an IPR; HW_MTPR writes one; never both in one grain.
# personality-flags (NOT a mutex group; any subset legal)
#   S_PalTru64  S_PalVms  S_PalLinux
#   Neither set:  personality-agnostic instruction (the common case).
#   One set:      CALL_PAL function code valid only under that personality.
#   Multiple set: encoding behaves identically (or with documented per-
#                 personality semantic divergence) under all flagged
#                 personalities.  Per AARM Table C-15 (alpha_arch_ref.txt
#                 ~line 47644) which gives the canonical OpenVMS / Tru64
#                 UNIX / Alpha Linux mnemonic for each function code.
#   Codegen emits PAL dispatch sub-tables (osf, vms, linux); the CPU
#   picks one at boot based on its configured PAL personality.  Non-
#   CALL_PAL instructions never set these flags.
# Stage consumers (informational; codegen verifies that each named stage
# in the 'stages' column has at least one handler that reads the flag)
# DE      decode stage; format/operand validation, OPCDEC dispatch
# GR      register/operand read; bypass-network selection
# EX      execute stage; ALU, branch resolution, divert decision, IPR access
# MEM     memory effect application; commit register writes
# WB      retirement; trap delivery, barrier ordering
# issue   pre-issue protection check (PAL mode requirement)
# trace   trace cache builder; classifies divert-capable and non-traceable grains
# Reserved bits
# Bits 22..23, 30..31, 39, 44..47, 55, 60..63 are intentionally left
# unassigned to leave headroom inside each category for future flags.
# (Bit 38 was reserved; now S_PalIntrinsic for inline-executed CALL_PAL
# functions like CSERVE.)
# Codegen treats reserved bits as zero and rejects rows that set them.
